flashcards.back: A concentric circle on a disk platter where data is stored.
Time taken for the desired sector to rotate under the read-write head.

flashcards.front: What is a key difference between HDDs and SSDs?
flashcards.back: HDDs use mechanical parts, while SSDs use flash memory for faster access.
Redundant Array of Independent Disks, combining drives for performance/reliability.
Time taken for the read-write head to move to the correct cylinder.
